openmeter/productcatalog/addon.purpose: Domain package for add-on lifecycle management: defines the Addon aggregate (with RateCards), the Service + Repository interfaces, typed errors, domain events, and status/deletion validators; its children split into adapter (Ent persistence), httpdriver (v1 HTTP), and service (validation, feature/taxcode resolution, event publishing). Primary constraint: domain types always carry both productcatalog.* base types and managed identity fields (NamespacedID, ManagedModel).

patterns.name: Addon aggregate embedding
patterns.description: Addon embeds models.NamespacedID, models.ManagedModel, productcatalog.AddonMeta, and RateCards; RateCard wraps productcatalog.RateCard with RateCardManagedFields (AddonID). Never use struct literals missing these fields.
patterns.example: type Addon struct { models.NamespacedID; models.ManagedModel; productcatalog.AddonMeta; RateCards RateCards }

patterns.name: RateCards.AsProductCatalogRateCards() for cross-type conversion
patterns.description: Never cast addon.RateCards to productcatalog.RateCards directly — call AsProductCatalogRateCards(), which extracts the inner productcatalog.RateCard from each wrapper.
patterns.example: func (c RateCards) AsProductCatalogRateCards() productcatalog.RateCards { for _, rc := range c { rcs = append(rcs, rc.RateCard) }; return rcs }

patterns.name: RateCard type-discriminator JSON
patterns.description: RateCard.UnmarshalJSON reads productcatalog.RateCardSerde.Type then switches FlatFeeRateCardType vs UsageBasedRateCardType to pick the concrete struct; new rate card types need a case here and in the ratecard test.
patterns.example: switch s.Type { case productcatalog.FlatFeeRateCardType: serde.RateCard = &productcatalog.FlatFeeRateCard{} }

patterns.name: Typed NotFoundError + draft validation options
patterns.description: Adapters return addon.NewNotFoundError(...) wrapping models.NewGenericNotFoundError (never raw entdb.IsNotFound); input structs embed inputOptions{IgnoreNonCriticalIssues} so drafts can pass with non-critical issues via WithSeverityOrHigher(ErrorSeverityCritical).
patterns.example: issues := models.AsValidationIssues(errors.Join(errs...)); if i.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues { issues = issues.WithSeverityOrHigher(models.ErrorSeverityCritical) }

patterns.name: Events via constructors, published inside the transaction
patterns.description: Lifecycle events (Create/Update/Delete/Publish/Archive) carry the full Addon pointer + UserID from session.GetSessionUserID(ctx); the service child publishes them inside transaction.Run so DB write and event stay consistent.
patterns.example: event := addon.NewAddonCreateEvent(ctx, created); s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event)

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (List/Create/Delete/Get/Update/Publish/Archive/Next) and input types with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: PublishAddonInput.Validate() uses a 30s jitter via clock.Now() for EffectiveFrom — do not substitute time.Now().

key_file_guides.role: Addon aggregate with Validate(), ValidateWith(), AsProductCatalogAddon().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Plans field is *[]Plan — only populated on expand; never assume non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: RateCard wrapping productcatalog.RateCard with RateCardManagedFields; custom Marshal/UnmarshalJSON dispatching on RateCardSerde.Type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New rate card types require a case in the UnmarshalJSON switch and the ratecard test.

key_file_guides.role: NotFoundError wrapping models.NewGenericNotFoundError; IsNotFound() for errors.As detection.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never return raw entdb.IsNotFound — always wrap via NewNotFoundError.

key_file_guides.role: Five lifecycle event structs; UserID sourced from session.GetSessionUserID(ctx).
key_file_guides.watch_for: AddonDeleteEvent.Validate() asserts Addon.DeletedAt != nil — soft-delete must be applied before constructing the event.

key_file_guides.role: Repository interface embedding entutils.TxCreator — mandatory for TransactingRepo in the adapter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Without TxCreator embedding the adapter cannot participate in ctx-bound transactions.

key_file_guides.role: IsAddonDeleted and HasAddonStatus models.ValidatorFunc[Addon] for service-layer guards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use via Addon.ValidateWith() instead of inline status checks.

anti_patterns: Returning raw entdb.IsNotFound errors — always wrap in addon.NewNotFoundError.
Using context.Background() in service or adapter code — always propagate ctx.
Querying add-ons without eager-loading rate cards (WithRatecards).
Publishing domain events outside a transaction closure — event failure should roll back the DB write.
Creating multiple non-deleted draft versions for the same key without checking HasDraft().

decisions.decision: RateCard is a separate managed type wrapping productcatalog.RateCard with AddonID.
decisions.rationale: Each rate card needs its own DB identity (NamespacedID) and foreign key (AddonID) so Ent can persist and eager-load them independently.

decisions.decision: inputOptions.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues enables partial validation for draft addons.
decisions.rationale: Drafts can be created with non-critical issues (e.g. features not yet registered); callers opt into relaxed validation that only fails on ErrorSeverityCritical.

decisions.decision: Tax code and feature resolution happen at the service layer, not the adapter.
decisions.rationale: resolveFeatures/resolveTaxCodes (GetOrCreateByAppMapping) are cross-domain orchestration that the persistence layer must stay free of.

code_examples.scenario: Publishing a domain event after a successful adapter write inside a transaction

openmeter/productcatalog/plan.purpose: Domain package for plan lifecycle management: defines Plan (with Phases and RateCards), Phase/RateCard managed types, typed errors, domain events, a custom JSON serializer, and the Service + Repository interfaces; children split into adapter (Ent), httpdriver (v1 HTTP), and service (validation, feature/taxcode resolution, events). Primary constraint: EffectivePeriod changes are only allowed via Publish/Archive, never via UpdatePlan.

patterns.name: Nested managed-type embedding
patterns.description: Plan = NamespacedID + ManagedModel + PlanMeta + []Phase; Phase = PhaseManagedFields{ManagedModel, NamespacedID, PlanID} + productcatalog.Phase; RateCard = productcatalog.RateCard + RateCardManagedFields{PhaseID}.
patterns.example: type Plan struct { models.NamespacedID; models.ManagedModel; productcatalog.PlanMeta; Phases []Phase }

patterns.name: EffectivePeriod zeroed in UpdatePlan
patterns.description: UpdatePlanInput embeds productcatalog.EffectivePeriod but the service must zero it before calling the adapter — status transitions only go through Publish/Archive.
patterns.example: // service: input.EffectivePeriod = productcatalog.EffectivePeriod{} before adapter.UpdatePlan

patterns.name: Custom Plan JSON serializer for polymorphic RateCards
patterns.description: serializer.go implements Plan.Marshal/UnmarshalJSON using alias types to avoid recursion, dispatching RateCards via the RateCardSerde type discriminator (flat_fee / usage_based). Any new field on Plan/Phase/RateCard must be reflected here.
patterns.example: json.Marshal(plan) // Plan.MarshalJSON dispatches through rateCardAlias

patterns.name: ValidatorFunc[Plan] status/deletion guards
patterns.description: validators.go provides IsPlanDeleted(at) and HasPlanStatus(statuses...) as composable models.ValidatorFunc[Plan] used via Plan.ValidateWith().
patterns.example: if err := p.ValidateWith(plan.HasPlanStatus(productcatalog.PlanStatusDraft)); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Typed NotFoundError + draft validation options
patterns.description: Adapters return plan.NewNotFoundError(...) (never raw Ent not-found); CreatePlanInput/UpdatePlanInput embed inputOptions{IgnoreNonCriticalIssues} with AsValidationIssues+WithSeverityOrHigher for relaxed draft validation.
patterns.example: return nil, plan.NewNotFoundError(plan.NotFoundErrorParams{Namespace: ns, Key: key, Version: v})

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (List/Create/Delete/Get/Update/Publish/Archive/Next) and input types with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: PublishPlanInput.Validate() uses a 30s jitter via pkg/clock.Now() — not time.Now().

key_file_guides.role: Plan aggregate with Validate(), ValidateWith(), AsProductCatalogPlan().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Addons field is *[]Addon — only populated on expand; never assume non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: Phase and PhaseManagedFields types with their validator funcs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PhaseManagedFields.Validate() asserts PlanID != '' — always populate PlanID when constructing a Phase in adapters.

key_file_guides.role: RateCard wrapping productcatalog.RateCard with PhaseID; custom Marshal/UnmarshalJSON dispatching on type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New rate card types require a case in UnmarshalJSON and the serializer switch.

key_file_guides.role: Custom JSON marshaler/unmarshaler for Plan using alias types to avoid recursion.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new field on Plan/Phase/RateCard must be added here; serializer_test.go covers round-trip and error paths.

key_file_guides.role: IsPlanDeleted and HasPlanStatus validator funcs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsPlanDeleted takes a time.Time check argument — use clock.Now() at the call site.

anti_patterns: Setting EffectivePeriod via UpdatePlan — it must be zeroed in the service; only Publish/Archive alter it.
Skipping resolveFeatures/resolveTaxCodes before persisting rate cards in the service.
Returning raw entdb.IsNotFound — always wrap in plan.NewNotFoundError.
Publishing events outside a transaction.Run closure — DB write and event publish must be atomic.
Editing openmeter/ent/db/ generated files — always regenerate with make generate.

decisions.decision: EffectivePeriod changes are strictly gated to Publish/Archive.
decisions.rationale: UpdatePlan zeroes EffectivePeriod so callers cannot manipulate plan status via update; status transitions are explicit state-machine operations.

decisions.decision: Custom JSON serializer on Plan instead of generated oapi-codegen types.
decisions.rationale: productcatalog.RateCard is a polymorphic interface; the serializer dispatches on the type discriminator to pick the concrete struct, which generated decoders cannot handle.

decisions.decision: Version auto-incremented in the service layer, not settable by callers.
decisions.rationale: Prevents callers from forging version numbers; the service computes the next version from existing versions at create/next time.

code_examples.scenario: Service mutation wrapping adapter call and event publish in transaction.Run

openmeter/productcatalog/planaddon.purpose: Domain package for plan-to-addon assignment (PlanAddon): defines the PlanAddon aggregate linking a full plan.Plan and addon.Addon with quantity/phase constraints, typed errors, domain events, and the Service + Repository interfaces; children split into adapter (Ent), httpdriver (v1 HTTP), and service (cross-entity validation + events).

patterns.name: PlanAddon embeds full plan.Plan + addon.Addon
patterns.description: PlanAddon = NamespacedID + ManagedModel + PlanAddonMeta + Plan plan.Plan + Addon addon.Addon; AsProductCatalogPlanAddon() converts to the base type. Always populate Plan and Addon fully before constructing.
patterns.example: type PlanAddon struct { models.NamespacedID; models.ManagedModel; productcatalog.PlanAddonMeta; Plan plan.Plan; Addon addon.Addon }

patterns.name: Dual-lookup inputs (ID or plan+addon pair)
patterns.description: Get/Update/Delete inputs support either an assignment ID or a PlanIDOrKey + AddonIDOrKey pair; if ID is empty both pair fields must be provided. Adapters must handle both paths.
patterns.example: if i.ID == "" { if i.PlanIDOrKey == "" || i.AddonIDOrKey == "" { return err } }

patterns.name: Typed NotFoundError with pair params
patterns.description: NewNotFoundError accepts namespace, ID, PlanIDOrKey, AddonIDOrKey; use IsNotFound() for errors.As detection. Message format differs from plan/addon ('plan add-on assignment not found ...').
patterns.example: return nil, planaddon.NewNotFoundError(planaddon.NotFoundErrorParams{Namespace: ns, PlanIDOrKey: planKey, AddonIDOrKey: addonKey})

patterns.name: Equaler-based idempotent updates
patterns.description: UpdatePlanAddonInput implements models.Equaler[PlanAddon] so the service can skip the adapter call when the patch produces no change.
patterns.example: if input.Equal(*existing) { return existing, nil }

patterns.name: Events carry PlanAddon pointer + UserID, published in tx
patterns.description: Three events (Create/Update/Delete) carry the PlanAddon and UserID from session; DeleteEvent.Validate() asserts DeletedAt != nil. Publish inside transaction.Run after every mutation.
patterns.example: event := planaddon.NewPlanAddonCreateEvent(ctx, created); s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event)

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (List/Create/Delete/Get/Update) and input types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreatePlanAddonInput requires non-empty PlanID and AddonID; Get/Update/Delete support both ID-only and plan+addon-pair lookup.

key_file_guides.role: PlanAddon aggregate with Validate() and AsProductCatalogPlanAddon().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate() calls both Plan.Validate() and Addon.Validate() — ensure both are fully populated before constructing.

key_file_guides.role: NotFoundError and IsNotFound wrapping models.NewGenericNotFoundError.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Message format includes 'plan add-on assignment not found' — differs from plan/addon error messages.

key_file_guides.role: Three lifecycle events; subsystem is 'planaddon'.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PlanAddonDeleteEvent.Validate() requires PlanAddon.DeletedAt != nil — soft-delete must precede event construction.

key_file_guides.role: Repository extends entutils.TxCreator; 5-method CRUD interface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TxCreator is required for TransactingRepo in the adapter layer.

key_file_guides.role: Test helpers (AssertPlanAddon*Equal) in the main package, not testutils.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Kept in the main package so adapter/service tests can import them without a separate testutils import.

anti_patterns: Checking plan/addon status (draft, active) inside the adapter — status validation belongs in the service layer.
Returning raw adapter-level errors without wrapping in planaddon.NewNotFoundError.
Calling adapter methods outside transaction.Run for writes — risks partial writes if event publish fails.
Hard-deleting PlanAddon rows instead of soft-deleting via SetDeletedAt.
Skipping eager-load of Plan and Addon edges after create/update — both must always be fully populated.

decisions.decision: PlanAddon embeds full plan.Plan and addon.Addon rather than just IDs.
decisions.rationale: Callers (subscription sync, billing) need the full plan and addon data without extra lookups; eager-loading at the adapter avoids N+1 queries.

decisions.decision: Both ID-based and plan+addon-pair lookups supported in Get/Update/Delete.
decisions.rationale: HTTP handlers and internal code may have either the assignment ID or only the plan/addon identifiers; supporting both avoids forcing a Get before Update.

code_examples.scenario: Service write with cross-entity validation and event publish in a transaction

openmeter/productcatalog/subscription.purpose: Bridge package (package plansubscription) connecting the productcatalog plan model to the subscription domain: defines PlanSubscriptionService (Create, Migrate, Change), PlanInput (ref vs inline discriminator), and Plan/Phase/RateCard adapter types implementing the subscription.Plan/PlanPhase/PlanRateCard interfaces; children split into http (v1 driver), service (concrete bridge), and testutils. All subscription DB writes go through subscriptionworkflow.Service, never directly through subscription.Service.

patterns.name: PlanInput dual-path discriminator
patterns.description: PlanInput holds either a *PlanRefInput (existing plan by key/version) or *plan.CreatePlanInput (inline spec). Use FromRef/FromInput setters; AsRef()/AsInput() return nil for the unused path; Validate() errors if both are nil.
patterns.example: var pi plansubscription.PlanInput; pi.FromRef(&PlanRefInput{Key: "pro", Version: lo.ToPtr(2)})

patterns.name: Adapter types implement subscription interfaces
patterns.description: plan.go defines Plan, Phase, RateCard wrappers implementing subscription.Plan/PlanPhase/PlanRateCard. Plan.GetPhases() accumulates StartAfter by summing phase durations in order — reordering Phases breaks timing.
patterns.example: p := &plansubscription.Plan{Plan: fetchedPlan.AsProductCatalogPlan(), Ref: &models.NamespacedID{ID: fetchedPlan.ID}}

patterns.name: All persistence delegated to WorkflowService
patterns.description: The service resolves and validates the plan, then calls subscriptionworkflow.Service.CreateFromPlan / ChangeToPlan / Migrate. No direct subscription.Repository or Ent calls in this package.
patterns.example: return s.workflowService.CreateFromPlan(ctx, workflowInput, &adaptedPlan)

patterns.name: zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase collapses earlier phases
patterns.description: When StartingPhase is set, phases before it have Duration zeroed (not deleted) before passing the plan to WorkflowService.
patterns.example: // service/create.go: zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase(plan, *request.StartingPhase)

patterns.name: Custom-plan discriminator sniff via marshal+unmarshal
patterns.description: In the http/ child, custom-plan detection marshals then unmarshals into a plan-specific struct rather than using oapi-codegen As* helpers, which succeed on wrong types and silently produce zero values.
patterns.example: // http/create.go: marshal body to JSON then unmarshal into plan-specific struct to detect type

key_file_guides.role: PlanSubscriptionService interface (Create, Migrate, Change) and request types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChangeSubscriptionRequest.StartingPhase only applies when PlanInput.AsRef() is non-nil; set both consistently.

key_file_guides.role: Plan/Phase/RateCard adapter types wrapping productcatalog types to implement subscription.Plan interfaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Plan.GetPhases() accumulates StartAfter by summing phase durations in order — any reordering of Phases breaks subscription timing.

anti_patterns: Calling subscription.Service directly for create/change/migrate — always route through PlanSubscriptionService or WorkflowService.
Calling plan.Service.GetPlan directly instead of the getPlanByVersion helper — the helper normalises NotFound to subscription.NewPlanNotFoundError.
Setting StartingPhase without calling zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase — earlier phases won't collapse.
Importing app/common from testutils — it must stay independent to avoid import cycles.
Adding persistence calls directly in service/service.go — all DB writes go through WorkflowService.

decisions.decision: PlanInput discriminator (ref vs inline) instead of separate Create methods.
decisions.rationale: Callers may subscribe to an existing stored plan or a transient inline spec without separate entry points; the discriminator validates exactly one path is set.

decisions.decision: Plan/Phase/RateCard adapter types implement subscription interfaces rather than using productcatalog types directly.
decisions.rationale: Subscription defines its own Plan/PlanPhase/PlanRateCard interfaces; the adapter types bridge productcatalog.Plan to them without circular imports.

code_examples.scenario: Resolving a plan by ref and delegating subscription creation to WorkflowService
